item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So. Like. This thing is basically Uncontained, right? Any time they Foundation can't locate an individual in time… say, because of vague "geopolitical complexities…" the kid will sense what's about to happen, and they might tell their parents. So it's not only Uncontained, it's leaking information about itself to the general population. It's essentially lifting the veil. It's just doing it very slowly.

At what point does the Foundation just try killing the parents? After all, if they parents aren't alive, they technically can't be afraid of the dark…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Without the benefit of the associated tale (will read shortly), I still have to say that this is fantastic. It would have worked better just leaving off at either All Khan (brrr) or one of the kids peeling through the cartons, but obviously wouldn't have had the same 1-2 half-hour punch. Still, very very enjoyable and an easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have mixed opinions on this one. The concept is good but the containment procedures are a bit lackluster. The list needs to be a bit shorter and more diversified . Anyway the accompanying tale does win me over so

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am hyped for August 9th now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since the authorship is no longer clear from this: this skip was written by me, Tufto, on my first account prior to my deleting it. You can find more of my stuff here.

item.title: Minor Grammar Gripe
item.content:encoded:
Just one issue I found during the addendum:

SCP-3553-A-8: I think know what the cartons are.

That should be "I think I know what the cartons are"? It's just a minor grammar issue, so I've taken the liberty of correcting it for you. Please feel free to yell at me if I fucked it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This and the tale the goes along with it is a work of art. (+1) i wish to know: will you be doing more on it? Are you thinking about it? or have you not decided?

I love the characterization, but the Final collapsible feels like it is trying a wee bit too hard to be emotional. (Don't really know what to say beyond that, even though i'd like too.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, no worries, I know it's not personal :p I did kinda rush my most recent three skips a bit, and I'm surprised they've done as well as they have done, frankly. I'm taking it slower next time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gah - I keep almost upvoting skips, and then finding they're all by you, Tufto. I swear it's nothing personal - and these are all very close, just not quite there for me. In this case, it's the fact that the story plays out a little too straight - ominous messages, kids disappear, no variation. I will give the tale a read - that might make the difference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah- Robert's DOB was an error. Shall fix.

Could you go into a bit more detail about why the experiment is unprofessional? It is largely an act of desperation, as there is apparently nothing they can do to prevent it; they're just attempting to stabilise, observe and physically hold Robert in as many ways as possible.

Why are so many cartons discovered years after their appearance ?

Because discovering messages written on single milk cartons all over two countries is nigh-impossible, especially since most will be destroyed. They're searching for a needle in a haystack; I think the fact that I've included so many found is pushing it a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, they've been trying to avoid that, as they are intending to save the child. But if they tried, some accident or error would prevent it; the disappearance will happen as described, and there's nothing they can do to stop it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One less child will be dragged to the dream-based gestalt seen on the associated tale, I suppose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The experiment in Addendum 1 seems a little unprofessional for the Foundation. Also in the description you mentioned that it was children under 16 years old who disappeared, but Robert's year of birth is given as 1994?

Why are so many cartons discovered years after their appearance ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What will happen if we kill the kid before the time so there is no kid to disappear?
or turn him/her into Allison Eckhart or use some other semantic SCPs so no longer such person exsit anymore?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lo and behold, SCP-3501

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope. There are several that appeared before the event happened; the cartons are always past-tense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
She disappeared from Castlemorton Common in Worcestershire 7 days ago.

Shouldn't the text be referencing an event in the future, not in the past?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was horrifying, Tufto. I'd read Endless Night, and this drew back the curtain on the other side quite nicely (for a given value of 'nicely'). As a parent, this hit me hard in what the kids today call the feels. Especially the experiment at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lulz " don't be afraid kid, we're just…Uhh.. Exorcising Demons! Yeah that's it! Nothing to worry about!"

Excuse of the year award goes to. .

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1; it started slow at first, but the implications are worrying in the best way possible.

By the way, is Ali Khan connected to SCP-1782 by any chance? Between the details given here and the reference in the tale, it very much reminded me of that skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're in the positives, Great job Tufto! :D

+1, I love how this turned out- and the Experiment Log still gets to me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really am taking a break from writing this time.

It's amazing how many times Kurt Vonnegut said the same thing. X3

item.title: Upboatttt
item.content:encoded:
Wow, you posted already?

Good job. You are definitely one of the rising authors that will make up the new generation of classics.

Take my hearty +1 :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really am taking a break from writing this time.

This skip shows the "other side" of my tale, "Some Are Born To Endless Night". However, I wanted to make it work on its own merits, so I haven't crosslinked. If you want to peel back the mystery and see what happens next, read the tale.

With thanks to the following:

taylor_itkin
Salem
Rimple
Blackfluid
Hippo
Flawed
derpydm
iveo1912
SpookyPizza
